title: IDOR (Insecure Direct Object Reference)
description: If the system doesn't properly validate user-supplied identifiers (e.g., object IDs, file names) when accessing resources, attackers could potentially manipulate these identifiers to gain unauthorized access to privileged resources or escalate their privileges.
attack_vector: Exploiting vulnerabilities in the application logic that allows attackers to bypass authorization checks and access resources they shouldn't have access to.
mitigations: Implement robust authorization checks for all data access requests.
Use indirect object references (e.g., GUIDs) instead of direct object references.
Enforce strict access control policies and regularly review user permissions.
